query: What demand charges apply to commercial customers with demand >500 kW in California, within PG&E's territory, based on the OpenEI tariffs data? Answer should contain service class name and demand charge for at least 2 classes
final_answer:
Using OpenEI’s PG&E commercial tariff records, the demand-based classes above 500 kW are:

| Service class | Applicability in OpenEI | Demand charge(s) listed in OpenEI | Source |
|---|---:|---|---|
| **B-19 Medium General Demand TOU (Primary) Mandatory** | 500–999 kW | **Seasonal/monthly demand charge: $29.11**; **TOU demand charges: $1.69, $8.54, $37.89** | OpenEI USURDB rate view: https://apps.openei.org/USURDB/rate/view/69de8ce0fcf14d499f019a5e |
| **E-20 Maximum demand of (1000 KW or more) (Primary)** | ≥1000 kW | **Seasonal/monthly demand charge: $27.82**; **TOU demand charges: $0, $0, $14.74, $17.62** | OpenEI USURDB rate view: https://apps.openei.org/USURDB/rate/view/62d5bbc2cc1444085b01ac87 |

Notes:
- Both are commercial PG&E tariffs in OpenEI’s USURDB. Source: the two OpenEI rate pages above.
- The B-19 record explicitly applies to **500–999 kW** customers, and the E-20 record explicitly applies to **1000 kW or more** customers. Source: the same two OpenEI pages above.

If you want, I can also pull the same comparison for **secondary** and **transmission** voltage versions of these PG&E classes from OpenEI.
